steps.title: Build the plausible interval
steps.content:
The estimated mean is $6.2$ hours, and the margin of error is $0.4$ hours. Subtract and add the margin of error.
$$6.2-0.4=5.8$$
$$6.2+0.4=6.6$$

steps.title: Interpret the interval
steps.content: The interval from $5.8$ to $6.6$ hours describes plausible values for the mean time studied by all students, not the study time of every individual student. Choices about all students, most students, or any one student's minimum time misuse the margin of error because the survey estimates an average for the whole population. The answer is D.
